tags.name: Insurance
tags.description: Claims, underwriting, compliance, and policy operations.

stacks.name: Twilio
stacks.description: SMS and voice infrastructure for reminders and alerts.

problems.title: Claims follow-up for independent adjusters
problems.tagline: Track documents, callbacks, and next actions without juggling inboxes.
problems.excerpt: Independent adjusters often manage claim follow-up from email threads, spreadsheets, and memory.
problems.summary: Independent adjusters need a lightweight workflow to track claim status, missing documents, and policyholder follow-up.
problems.description: Adjusters working across multiple carriers and claims often lose time to repeated callbacks, scattered documentation, and manual reminders. A focused workflow tool could reduce dropped follow-up and make status visibility much clearer.
problems.targetUser: Independent insurance adjusters and small claims teams
problems.painPoint: Critical next steps get buried across inboxes, phone notes, and spreadsheets, making claims slower and harder to manage consistently.
problems.suggestedMvp: Claim tracker, status pipeline, missing-document checklist, callback reminders, and simple activity logs.
problems.demandSignalsSummary: Operations discussions frequently mention callback volume, missing paperwork, and fragmented claim status tracking.
problems.riskNotes: Workflow varies by carrier, so the first version should stay flexible and avoid deep system dependencies.

evidences.title: Adjusters discuss callback overload
evidences.summary: Operators describe losing track of claims due to scattered notes and follow-up steps.
evidences.snippet: Manual reminders and inbox search are still common ways to stay on top of open claims.
